summary: Transcript parser uses two-pass JSONL extraction with workflow segmentation at twining_assemble boundaries
context: Phase 17 needed to parse real Claude Code session JSONL logs into the same ScorerInput format used by synthetic scenarios. JSONL files contain tool_use blocks in assistant messages and tool_result blocks in user messages, linked by tool_use_id.
rationale: Two-pass extraction (first collect tool_use, then match tool_result) handles the JSONL structure where calls and results are in separate messages. Segmentation at twining_assemble boundaries creates natural workflow units matching how agents actually work. MCP name normalization via split('__').pop() handles both prefix patterns observed in real transcripts.

alternatives.reason_rejected: tool_result blocks appear after tool_use in separate messages — would need lookahead or buffering that adds complexity without benefit
Research showed all tool calls appear in the same JSONL file regardless of parentUuid — traversal is unnecessary overhead
